indexing_family.name: h3
indexing_family.rationale: H3 hexagonal cells give every cell six neighbors that share full edges, removing the neighbor-discontinuity artifacts of geohash quadrants. The JS/WASM ecosystem (h3-js) is the strongest of the three candidates evaluated. Resolutions 6 (district), 8 (neighborhood), 10 (building cluster), and 12 (lot) cover the zoom range a civic atlas needs.

viewport_cache_key.canonical_form: Stable JSON serialization of the fields above, hashed to a short opaque key.
invalidation_triggers: Source freshness change for any source id in the layer
Read model rebuild for the affected layer
Review state transition for any contained civic object

rusty_red_boundaries.label: Viewport cache (hot)
notes: Hot cache for repeated viewport queries during a session.
Cache misses fall back to the canonical read model path.

rusty_red_boundaries.label: Scene hydration cache
notes: Hydrated scene state for fast re-entry to the same scene during a session.
Discarded on tab close. Not a publication artifact.

rusty_red_boundaries.label: Nearby civic object lookup
notes: Short-lived cache for tap-to-explore and dossier hover affordances.
Never the source of truth for a public dossier.

rust_preprocessing_lanes.label: GeoParquet partitioner
notes: Partitions reviewed read models into atlas-node-scoped GeoParquet files.
Runs offline; results land in the public read-model origin.

rust_preprocessing_lanes.label: PMTiles packer
notes: Packs reviewed basemap layers into PMTiles archives.
Output is the basemap_archive read-model role.
Rusty Red is hot state. None of these boundaries are canonical truth.
Indexing family is proposed; revisit at the design brainstorm before promoting beyond proposed.
Rust preprocessing lanes run offline. Public page load must not depend on them being available.
